steps.title: Restate the criterion visually
steps.content: The predicted $y$-value comes from the line of best fit. The actual $y$-value comes from the dot. The line predicts a greater $y$-value than the actual data point exactly when the dot is below the line.

steps.title: Count the dots that lie below the line
steps.content: Scan left to right and count only dots below the line. The below-line dots occur near $x\approx2.5$, $3.3$, $4.1$, $4.9$, $7.3$, and $9.0$. That is $6$ data points.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Since $6$ dots have actual values below the prediction line, the line predicts a greater $y$-value than the actual $y$-value for $6$ points. The answer is 6.
